# semantic

**Instance Property**

The semantic meaning for the material property’s value.

## Declaration

```swift
var semantic: MDLMaterialSemantic { get set }
```

## Discussion

A material semantic determines how the material property’s value should be interpreted to produce a specific surface appearance in rendering. For example, the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLMaterialSemantic/baseColor] semantic defines the default color of each pixel on a rendered surface before shading effects are applied.

When Model I/O  loads materials from an [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLAsset] object, it automatically selects the appropriate standard semantics for the surface rendering descriptions in the asset file.

## Using a Material Property

- **name**: A descriptive name for the material property.
- **type**: The data type stored in the material property’s value.

items.description: A descriptive name for the material property.
items.name: name
